rows.description: Computus Gn. Daysiter Rectoris a secundo die novemb. 1708 ad eundem diem 1709.
rows.notes: Clear header text at top of page. No amounts present. This is the title/date range for the ledger account.
Opening descriptor phrase for first account section. Latin formula indicating the beginning of remaining balance. No amounts here.
Clear three-digit amount. Vertical column lines are well-defined. Shillings (17) and pence (10) both within valid ranges. Minor handwriting flourish on '4' in pounds but legible.
Section subheader introducing arrears entries. No amounts present.
Numbers clearly positioned in column. '60' in pounds column, followed by two zeros. Handwriting is neat. Abbreviation 'anno' is common in these records.
All three values clearly legible. Shillings (19) at top boundary of valid range (0-19). Pence (9) clearly within valid range. Column alignment is consistent with adjacent rows.
Abbreviated text ('p.' likely 'pount' or similar). Amounts clearly visible: 20 pounds, zeros for shillings and pence. Minor legibility on abbreviation but amounts unambiguous.
Clear three-value row. Numbers well-spaced and aligned with column boundaries. No ambiguities in digit reading.
Entry with abbreviations ('Ry', 'mich' for Michaelmas). Amounts clearly legible: 10 pounds, two zeros. Column alignment is consistent.
Entry with zero in pounds column. Shillings (3) and pence (8) both clearly visible and valid. Handwriting in this row is neat and consistent with page style.
Section subheader or descriptor. No amounts. Text is legible but somewhat compressed. Appears to introduce next group of entries.
Place name header. Single word, clearly legible. No amounts. Introduces the Thropp section entries.
Entry descriptor for capon payment. Text is handwritten and somewhat ornate. No amounts visible here — this appears to be a label for grouped entries below.
Small amount with fractional pence. The fraction symbol after '2' appears to be 'ob' (half-penny = 0.5). Pence digit (2) is clear. Zero pounds and shillings. Valid currency.
Legible entry with fractional pence. '5' clearly in shillings column (valid: 0-19). Pence '3' with 'ob' fraction mark. Zero in pounds column. Currency validates.
Another fractional entry. Pence digit appears to be '9' with 'ob' fraction. Shillings (3) valid. Zero pounds. Column alignment is consistent with similar entries above.
Clear three-value entry. Shillings (12) valid. Pence '3' with 'ob' fraction = 0.5. Zero pounds. All values within currency ranges.
Latin phrase 'quincquennio et Dimid.' (five years and a half). This is a section label/descriptor with no amounts. Handwriting is slightly ornate but readable.
Entry label/name entry. No amounts. Introduces Hughes entries below. Text is legible.
Entry label with period specification. No amounts. Introduces next section.
Entry with all-zero amounts. Numbers are clearly visible in their columns. This may represent a placeholder or complete payment.
Clear three-value entry. Shillings (15) within valid range. Pence is zero. Zero in pounds. All values clearly legible and aligned.
Three-value entry with low shillings amount (1). Pence is zero. Zero pounds. Column alignment is consistent. All currency values validate.
All-zero entry. Text is legible. Amounts are clearly positioned in columns, all showing zero.

CRITICAL ISSUE: Pence shows '18' which exceeds valid range (0-11). This indicates column misalignment. Re-examining the position: the digit sequence appears to be '04 | 18 | 7/4'. The '4' is in pounds, making shillings '18' (also invalid). Likely misreading. Given handwriting style and neighboring entries, reinterpreting as: 0 pounds | 4 shillings | 1 pence with '8' as fraction (likely '3q' = three farthings = 0.75). High uncertainty.

Pence digit shows '12' which violates currency rules (max 11). Column misalignment suspected. The brace { indicates this is a sub-item. Looking at alignment with row above and below, this may be: 0 pounds | 2 shillings | but pence is ambiguous. Given context, interpreting as: 0 | 2 | incomplete or misread pence. Confidence is low.
Sub-item under Swithhell brace. Shillings (6) valid. Pence '7' with what appears to be '3q' or similar fraction mark = 0.75. Zero pounds. Currency validates when interpreted this way.

VALIDATION ERROR: Pence shows '17' which exceeds 11. Column misalignment. Re-examining: sequence is '04 | 17 | 2'. If '04' spans pounds/shillings boundary, this would be invalid. Likely reading error. Given surrounding entries pattern, best reinterpretation is: 0 | 4 | but pence ambiguous. Low confidence due to systematic reading issues in this section.
Clear three-value entry. Shillings (1) valid. Pence '9' with 'ob' fraction = 0.5. Pounds (10) legible. All currency values validate. Good column alignment.
Clear entry. Shillings (8) valid. Pence '2' with 'ob' fraction = 0.5. Zero pounds. Column alignment consistent with well-formed entries. Currency validates.
Entry header/label for South Newton. No amounts. Text is legible. Appears to introduce a subsection or account group.
Entry label. No amounts visible. Handwriting is clear. Likely a sub-entry label introducing amounts on line below.
All-zero entry. Amounts are clearly positioned. Latin period designation 'guadyrennio' (likely 'quad-anno' or similar). Column alignment is consistent.
Clear three-value entry. Shillings (4) valid. Pence is zero. Zero pounds. All values clearly visible and properly aligned.
Entry with fractional pence. Shillings (5) valid. Pence appears to be '0' with a '1/4' or 'q' marking = 0.25. Zero pounds. Currency validates.
Similar structure to row above. Shillings (5) valid. Pence '0' with '1/4' fraction = 0.25. Zero pounds. Handwriting is consistent with page style. Currency validates.
All-zero entry. Final item before total line. Amounts clearly visible in all three columns, all showing zero.

Final totals line at bottom of page. Clearly marked by underline and prominent positioning. Shillings (19) at top boundary of valid range. Pence '9' with 'ob' fraction = 0.5. All values crystal clear and properly aligned. This is the sum of all entries above.
